Custom controller authoring cleanup: consolidate reusable controller helpers into a single discoverable module under ENGINE/runtime/animation/controllers/shared (including custom_asset_controller.hpp and related default behavior helpers), and document a clear extension contract for user controllers created from the Animation Editor template flow. Implementation direction: define one unified base behavior interface/class (lifecycle/update hooks, default callable functions, and utility accessors), make the generated base custom controller include/derive from it, and ensure DefaultCustomController dispatch has matching implementations for every documented default hook. Constraints/cleanup: remove duplicate helper paths, keep backward compatibility for existing custom controller keys referenced by asset metadata, and update template/scaffold code so newly generated controllers are fully wired without manual patching.

Weighted Random range editor UX pass: improve the slider interaction in the Asset Info editor so it supports both high-precision edits and fast large-range movement. Target the weighted-random control rendering/input path in the Asset Info stack (DM slider widget + panel that hosts weighted random values). Implementation direction: add velocity-scaled dragging (fine movement near cursor origin, accelerated deltas at larger drag distance), normalize visual feedback (handle position/label refresh), and add an optional expanded popup editor for precise numeric entry and broader drag space. Constraints/cleanup: preserve current serialized value format, keep keyboard/manual numeric entry behavior consistent with other sliders, and avoid regressions in min/max clamping.

Asset Info Stack anchor-point depth edit behavior: in RoomEditor/asset-info anchor editing, change mouse-wheel depth adjustments from fractional percentage steps to fixed 1-pixel world-depth increments per wheel tick. Relevant systems: anchor edit input handling in RoomEditor, anchor value synchronization back to Asset Info UI fields, and payload persistence in asset animation metadata. Constraints/cleanup: preserve existing sign/direction semantics, keep snapping rules unchanged for non-depth axes, and ensure repeated wheel input cannot accumulate float drift.

Dynamic asset efficiency rendering optimization: for assets whose depth is between efficiency_depth and cull_depth, pause animation frame advancement while still rendering them; above efficiency_depth keep assets fully opaque and skip fog interpolation work. Replace current horizon thinning behavior with fog-based attenuation in the same dynamic efficiency pipeline. Relevant systems: RoomEditor/runtime dynamic asset visibility filtering, animation tick/update loop, and fog application path used by dynamic asset rendering. Implementation direction: introduce a depth-band state (full, paused+fogged, culled), gate animation update calls for paused band, and compute fog only where needed (between efficiency and cull thresholds). Constraints/cleanup: keep cull behavior unchanged, avoid per-frame extra allocations in depth classification, and ensure paused assets resume animation deterministically when re-entering active depth.

UI relocation task: move the 'Near Fog' control out of the Dynamic Assets panel and into the Camera panel in Dev Mode Room Editor. Relevant files/systems: RoomEditor panel construction/layout, camera tools panel widget bindings, and settings persistence for dynamic rendering controls. Implementation direction: rebind the near-fog value source to the camera panel control, remove duplicate control wiring from dynamic-assets UI, and preserve the same runtime variable/serialization key. Constraints/cleanup: maintain existing default value/loading behavior, avoid creating two active controls for the same field, and keep panel spacing/ordering consistent with current camera controls.

Asset Info Stack > Movement Editor (non-zero DY handle split): in RoomEditor movement edit mode, prevent floor reprojection of elevated points by separating interaction handles: a ground crosshair handle edits X/Z only (DX/DZ), and an elevated handle (or wheel-Y path) edits Y only (DY). Relevant systems: movement point hit testing, drag routing, world/screen transform helpers, and RoomMovementToolsPanel numeric sync. Implementation direction: add distinct hit targets and drag modes keyed by selected point DY, route X/Z drags through floor projection only, and isolate Y updates from horizontal coordinates. Constraints/cleanup: preserve snap-to-grid behavior for X/Z when snapping is enabled, keep DY==0 behavior unchanged, and guarantee no unintended Y reset during horizontal drag operations.

Asset Info Stack > Movement Editor path binding bug: when animation or path selection changes, always bind UI state to the selected animation’s real movement_paths payload (never stale paths or synthetic 0,0,0 rows). Relevant systems: RoomEditor movement_edit_ subview state, RoomMovementToolsPanel path list callbacks, and FrameEditState movement payload parse/build helpers. Implementation direction: on animation change, rebuild available path list from current animation JSON/runtime payload, clamp selected_path_index to valid range, and refresh active frame/point views from that exact path. Constraints/cleanup: eliminate cross-animation/path state leakage, keep movement path counts synchronized with movement_paths JSON, and only show empty defaults when authored path data is truly empty.

Asset Info Stack > Movement Editor wheel Y workflow: ensure mouse-wheel vertical editing updates DY live without modifying X/Z, and make XY-grid overlay mode explicit during vertical adjustment. Relevant systems: RoomEditor movement input handling, movement point transform mutation, overlay/grid rendering, and RoomMovementToolsPanel Y field synchronization. Implementation direction: on wheel edit enter XY grid mode (resolution matched to floor-grid spacing), apply per-tick Y changes at fixed X/Z, and keep XY mode active until click-to-exit returns to floor grid view. Constraints/cleanup: if snapping ON, each wheel step resolves to next snapped 3D Y level at current X/Z; if snapping OFF, each tick changes Y by exactly 1 unit; transitions between floor and XY modes must be reversible and visually clear.
